# Rosalind Problem: Using_the_Spectrum_Graph_to_Infer_Peptides (Visual Basic Solution)

## Problem Understanding

This problem involves reconstructing peptides from mass spectrometry data using spectrum graphs. We need to find all possible peptides that could produce a given spectrum.

## Solution Approach

The approach uses a graph-based method where:
1. Build a spectrum graph from the given spectrum
2. Find all paths from source to sink that represent valid peptides
3. Convert mass values to amino acid letters

## Visual Basic Implementation

```vb
Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Linq

Module SpectrumGraphPeptideInference
    ' Amino acid masses (rounded to integers)
    Private ReadOnly AMINO_ACID_MASSES As Dictionary(Of String, Integer) = New Dictionary(Of String, Integer) From {
        {"A", 71}, {"C", 103}, {"D", 115}, {"E", 129}, {"F", 147},
        {"G", 57}, {"H", 137}, {"I", 113}, {"K", 128}, {"L", 113},
        {"M", 131}, {"N", 114}, {"P", 97}, {"Q", 128}, {"R", 156},
        {"S", 87}, {"T", 101}, {"V", 99}, {"W", 186}, {"Y", 163}
    }
    
    ' Reverse mapping: mass -> amino acids
    Private ReadOnly MASS_TO_AMINO_ACIDS As Dictionary(Of Integer, List(Of String)) = New Dictionary(Of Integer, List(Of String))()
    
    ' Spectrum graph representation
    Private graph As Dictionary(Of Integer, List(Of Integer)) = New Dictionary(Of Integer, List(Of Integer))()
    
    Sub Main()
        ' Example spectrum (replace with actual input)
        Dim spectrum As List(Of Integer) = New List(Of Integer) From {0, 113, 128, 186, 244, 299, 313, 329, 343, 357, 425, 440, 454, 468, 482, 505, 520, 534, 548, 562, 576, 590, 604, 618, 632, 646,
